title: Pilosella cinerosiformis
taxonomy: Pilosella cinerosiformis is classified in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, and its specific classification includes the subgenus Eunomus. Within the family Asteraceae, Pilosella cinerosiformis is a member of the tribe Cichorieae. The species Pilosella cinerosiformis is also known to be closely related to other species in the Pilosella genus. This classification helps in identifying and differentiating Pilosella cinerosiformis from other related species.

intro: Pilosella cinerosiformis, also known as a species of hawkweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. Its bioactive constituents include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pilosella cinerosiformis is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine and homeopathy.
